commit.message:
Rework Motherlode: 0.2 DRILL/round mint, threshold buyback, burn(), graceful supply exhaustion + reduce RPC polling

Contracts:
- DrillToken: add burn() to free supply, make gameMint/creditDrill graceful (cap instead of revert)
- Motherlode: add creditFromGame() for direct DRILL accounting from game mints
- DrillGame: mint 0.2 DRILL to motherlode each round, auto-buyback at 0.05 ETH threshold
- New emission: 1.3 DRILL/round (1.0 survivors + 0.1 team + 0.2 motherlode)
- 124 tests passing including new burn, graceful supply, buyback, and near-max-supply tests

Frontend:
- Remove watchContractEvent HTTP pollers (~120 RPC calls/min saved)
- Increase all polling intervals (10-30s → 15-60s), ~70% RPC reduction
- Update ABIs and config for new constants/events

commit.message:
Fix duplicate image key bug in listing editor by using unique IDs

Images with the same filename caused React key collisions, leading to
wrong counts, stale DOM nodes, and duplicate cover badges.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: move C++ runtime to --extra-libs for correct link order

FFmpeg's configure link test uses: $LDFLAGS ... $TMPO $libs $extralibs
The C++ runtime (-lstdc++/-lc++) must come AFTER libtesseract.a in link
order so GNU ld resolves C++ symbols referenced by the static library.
Move from --extra-ldflags (maps to $LDFLAGS, before libs) to
--extra-libs (maps to $extralibs, after libs).

commit.message:
Fix critical PWA data loss bug: prevent sync errors from marking as complete

This fix resolves a critical issue where pro users were losing all data on PWA, particularly on mobile devices with flaky network connections.

Root cause: When cloud sync failed with a network error, the system incorrectly marked sync as "done", causing users to be forced through onboarding which overwrote their existing data.

Changes:
- sync-context: Only mark initialSyncDone=true on successful sync, not on error
- sync-context: Add 30-second safety timeout to prevent infinite loading
- sync-engine: Add exponential backoff retry logic (1s, 2s, 4s) for network failures
- OnboardingSimplified: Add protection to skip onboarding if user already has data

Impact: PWA will now properly retry failed syncs instead of showing empty state, preventing accidental data overwrites through onboarding flow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Upgrade to C++17 and document perf optimization attempts

Upgrade C++ standard from C++14 to C++17 (clean, no perf change).

Optimization attempts that were profiled and rejected:
- Pre-computed neighbor table for markConnected: ~5% slower due to
  extra indirection costing more than bounds checks save
- PGO/FDO (-fprofile-generate/-fprofile-use): 8% slower, conflicts
  with LTO
- Incremental make/unmake undo (save 1KB bitset copy): 12% slower
  because LTO changed inlining decisions for the new function shape
- Fixed-size stack array in markConnected: 10% slower (stack frame
  pollution vs static vector)
- Flood-fill skip heuristic (61% of flood-fills don't disconnect):
  incorrect — having enemy neighbors doesn't prove home-edge
  reachability
- C++20: obstack library uses removed std::allocator::pointer

Current NPS: ~3.1M (7.3x from 421K baseline). Profile is dominated
by inherent algorithmic work: markConnected flood-fill (47%) and
bitset ORs for move generation (12%).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v5: multi-pin ADC scan + settle delay to find correct BAT_PIN

BAT_PIN 0 (GPIO0) consistently returns ~285 mV for a 4.163V battery;
expected is ~1040 mV with a 1/4 divider. Root causes identified:

1. Wrong pin: GPIO0 is not the battery divider output. Physical A0 on
   XIAO ESP32-C6 = GPIO2. Changed BAT_PIN to 2 as best guess.

2. No settle delay: large resistor divider presents high impedance to
   the ADC sample-and-hold cap. 10 dummy reads are insufficient; the
   cap needs ~2 seconds to charge, which is why boot screen showed 0.

Changes:
- BAT_PIN: 0 → 2 (GPIO2 = physical A0 on XIAO ESP32-C6)
- Boot screen replaced with 4-pin simultaneous scan (pins 0,1,2,3)
- 2-second settle delay added before scan reads
- "Settling ADC..." message shown immediately so display is not blank
- EMA seeded from post-settle PIN 2 reading (not pre-settle)
- Scan shows all 4 candidates; * marks current BAT_PIN
- User should find pin with ~1040mV reading and update BAT_PIN

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: one-hot Dory commits with homomorphic hint combining (#16)

* perf: one-hot batch affine additions for Dory commits

Replace dense K×T field element expansion + Pippenger MSM for one-hot
polynomials (38 of 40 committed polys) with sparse index arrays + batch
affine point additions using Montgomery's batch inversion trick.

- Add batchInversion to MontgomeryField (1 inversion + 3(n-1) muls)
- Add batch_affine.zig with batchG1Additions/batchG1AdditionsMulti
- Add commitOneHotWithPool to Dory (direct G1 basis point summing)
- Store onehot_indices ([][]?u8) instead of dense witness_polys for
  sparse polynomials, reducing memory from ~1.2GB to ~2.4MB
- Sparse joint poly construction: O(T) per poly instead of O(K×T)

All 8 programs verified against upstream Jolt verifier.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: use projective accumulation instead of naive affine adds

Replace batchG1Additions (1 field inversion per add) with projective
accumulation via addAffine (no inversions) + single toAffine at end.
For rows with ~64 points this eliminates ~63 expensive Fermat inversions.

primes_large: 33.8s → 17.4s (was 26.1s before one-hot change)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: homomorphic hint combining for Stage 8 Dory opening

Cache G1 row commitments from the initial Dory commit phase and combine
them homomorphically (Σ γ^k · rows_k[i]) using GLV scalar multiplication
instead of recomputing row commitments from scratch via full MSM in Stage 8.

primes_large: 26.1s → 15.7s (40% total speedup)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: replace batch affine with projective accumulation, extract Dory row index building

Remove batch_affine.zig and switch one-hot Dory commits to projective
accumulation. Extract buildOneHotRowIndexSlices for reuse, add
combineRowCommitmentHints for homomorphic hint combining, simplify
one-hot index assertions, add Fp12.isOne(), and add tests for batch
inversion and one-hot commit correctness.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>


---------

commit.message:
Increase demand card count to 120 to match board game event probability

The deck was 40 demand + 20 event cards (33% event rate). The physical
board game has ~120 demand + 20 event cards (~14% event rate). This
change matches the real game's ratio.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

SEO, performance en accessibility optimalisaties

SEO:
- Duplicate JSON-LD verwijderd van homepage (WebSite, Organization, Store
  staan al in root layout — Google zag dubbele schemas)
- Organization aggregateRating verwijderd (niet geldig op Organization type)
- Organization logo gefixt (was OG banner, nu echte logo.svg)
- Store paymentAccepted: array → string (Schema.org spec)
- Store addressLocality: "Nederland" → "Enter" (correcte plaats)
- Product OG type: article → weggelaten (beter voor e-commerce)
- robots.txt: /pokemon-kaarten en /collectie toegevoegd (duplicate content)

commit.message:
Render rivers as smooth, naturally meandering curves

Replace straight-line river rendering with Catmull-Rom splines and
deterministic pseudo-random meander points for a more organic look.
River crossing detection remains unchanged (polyline-based).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove unused Brain import from AICopilotPage

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resilient embedding job — guaranteed self-chain, throttle handling, per-product recovery

Root causes of stale jobs identified and fixed:

1. getTextEmbedding() now retries on ThrottlingException with backoff
   and returns null instead of throwing (prevents batch-killing errors)
2. computeEmbeddings() uses Promise.allSettled — one product failure
   no longer crashes entire batch; failed products auto-retry next batch
3. Self-chain is GUARANTEED via inner try/catch around loop — after()
   fires even after errors (except user stop/pause or job completion)
4. Pipeline fetchNextBatch wrapped in try/catch inside Promise.all
5. Self-chain fetch has 15s AbortController timeout
6. EMBEDDING_BATCH_SIZE: 40→20 (80→40 concurrent Bedrock calls,
   staying within AWS 50 TPS limit)
7. Batch sizes: 40→20 (images), 60→40 (text-only)
8. Heartbeat TTL: 90s→180s (synced in stats endpoint)

commit.message:
v4: restore analogReadResolution(12) and V_DIVIDER=4.0

User measured 4.163V at JST while display showed 0.60V (v3 regression).

Root cause: removing analogReadResolution(12) in v3 left the ADC
un-initialized on this BSP version, causing ~300 mV garbage readings.
The GitHub halving bug (#8999, #11041, #11324) is BSP-version-specific
and does not affect this board. analogReadResolution(12) is required
for correct ADC hardware initialisation here.

Empirical calibration confirmed:
  multimeter 4.163V → ADC ~1040 mV → 1040 × 4.0 / 1000 = 4.16V ✓
  Therefore V_DIVIDER = 4.0 (1/4 resistor divider on Expansion Board v1.1)

Changes:
- Restore analogReadResolution(12) in setup() with detailed warning comment
- Revert V_DIVIDER from 2.0 → 4.0 with empirical justification
- Update readBatRaw() comment to reflect that the call IS required
- Boot screen retained for ongoing calibration verification
- All v3 improvements kept (EMA, LiPo curve, (bool)Serial USB detection)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: redact API keys from exception tracebacks in logs (#283)

PR #264 redacted keys from httpx log messages, but exception
tracebacks (e.g. httpx.HTTPStatusError with ?key=... in the URL)
bypassed the regex because loguru renders {exception} separately.

Add a loguru patcher that sanitizes both message text and exception
args before rendering, covering all output paths (text, JSON, stdlib
intercept). Also apply _redact_sensitive to the JSON sink's
traceback.format_exception output as a defense-in-depth measure.

Fixes #282

commit.message:
fix(updater): use .default for ESM-only update-notifier package

update-notifier v7+ is ESM-only. require() returns { __esModule, default }
so calling it directly as a function throws TypeError, silently caught,
leaving notifier=null and update checks never running.

commit.message:
ci: link C++ runtime for Tesseract static linking on all targets

Tesseract is a C++ library with a C API (capi.h). When FFmpeg's
configure tests linking via the C compiler, C++ symbols from
libtesseract.a are unresolved unless the C++ runtime is explicitly
linked. Add -lstdc++ (Linux/mingw) or -lc++ (macOS/llvm-mingw)
to extra-ldflags on all platforms.

commit.message:
feat: Tdarr Flows pipeline replacing classic plugin stack

Classic plugin stacks have a known finalization bug (Issue #1133) where
the server handles file replacement instead of the node. Flows fix this
by wrapping the same Migz+Boosh plugins in the Flow framework with an
explicit replaceOriginalFile step. Also bumps bitrate_cutoff to 4000.

commit.message:
feat: download monitor stuck-complete detection + force recheck

Torrents at >=99% with 0 seeds can have stale piece maps. Instead of
purging immediately, force a recheck first and track the hash. If still
stuck next cycle, then purge. Prevents removing torrents that just
need a piece map refresh.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: migrate Overseerr to Seerr v3.1.0 + Tdarr ulimits

- Replace linuxserver/overseerr with ghcr.io/seerr-team/seerr
- Update volume mount path (/config -> /app/config)
- Add init: true for proper signal handling
- Bump start_period to 60s for Seerr's slower startup
- Add ulimits (nofile 65536/524288) to Tdarr node for large libraries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: autoheal sidecar for VPN-dependent download clients

Watchtower updates to Gluetun break qBit/SABnzbd because they share
network_mode: "service:gluetun" and Watchtower doesn't respect
depends_on ordering on recreation. Download clients come up before
the tunnel is established, lose connectivity, and sit dead.

- Add willfarrell/autoheal sidecar to monitor labeled containers
- Label qBit and SABnzbd with autoheal=true
- Change health checks to test tunnel connectivity (ping 1.1.1.1)
  in addition to web UI, so a dead tunnel marks containers unhealthy
- Bump start_period to 90s and timeout to 15s to account for tunnel
  establishment time
- Autoheal checks every 60s, restarts unhealthy containers automatically
